name: full-pipeline
description: Full idea-to-production pipeline (v4.0.0 — TASK-0073..0080 expansion): cost estimate (HITL) → requirements → clarify [→ optional spec-debate] [→ optional pre-arch research] → architecture [→ optional arch-debate] → UX/DB design → design system ∥ dependency graph → planning [→ optional dual-model plan-debate consensus] → phase-4b-research (conditional on uncertainty markers) → task DAG → impact analysis → phase-4c parallel 4-reviewer dispatch → phase-4d confidence scoring → phase-4e conditional debate → phase-4f finding aggregation → phase-4g change requests → contract freeze → phase-4h validation (revision loop, HITL@3) → code generation → guard layer → quality + testing → cross-artifact consistency → RTM → completeness audit → drift detection → defect management (conditional) → deployment → export + docs + post-run cost report. TASK-0078: reduced to 4 mandatory HITL gates (GATE-001..004). Set hitl_preset:'full' to restore all 18 legacy gates.

phases.label: Token Cost Estimate
phases.note: FEATURE-018: Pre-run token cost estimate with HITL approval. User approves (YES), rejects (NO — aborts cleanly), or reduces scope (REDUCE). Set pipeline_config.ci_mode: true to skip. Runs as the very first step before any other skill executes.

phases.label: Clarification (HITL)
phases.note: FEATURE-012: Identifies ambiguous requirements and suspends for user input. Auto-advances when no ambiguities found. Set skip_clarify:true to bypass in CI.

phases.label: Debate Preparation — Advocate + Skeptic (parallel)
phases.note: FEATURE-013: Runs two requirement-analyzer instances in parallel — one as advocate (finds opportunities and value) and one as skeptic (challenges assumptions and finds gaps) — to produce the two inputs required by debate-synthesizer. Only active when pipeline_config.debate_requirements: true.

config.system_prompt_prefix: Analyze as an advocate: find opportunities and value in these requirements.
Analyze as a skeptic: challenge assumptions, find gaps, and identify risks.

phases.label: Spec Debate (optional)
phases.note: FEATURE-013: Runs advocate+skeptic analyzer instances in parallel, then synthesizes a debate-hardened requirements set. Enable with pipeline_config.debate_requirements: true.

phases.label: Technology Research (optional)
phases.note: FEATURE-009: Generates structured alternatives/pros-cons/rationale artifact before architect makes technology choices. Enable with pipeline_config.research_enabled: true or when requirement-analyzer emits technology_research_needed: true.

phases.label: Design System + Dependency Graph
phases.note: TASK-0054: design-system-generator and dependency-analyzer have no data dependency on each other — run concurrently to save one sequential phase.

phases.label: Planning — Model A (Strategic Planner)
phases.note: Phase numbering gap: phase-3 is intentionally reserved. The design layer (phase-2b + phase-2c) occupies the logical phase-3 slot. Numbering skips to phase-4 to preserve roadmap compatibility. When debate_plan is enabled, this phase runs as Model A (Strategic Planner) in parallel with phase-4-planning-b. When debate_plan is false (default), this is the sole planning pass.

config.system_prompt_prefix_condition: pipeline_config.debate_plan === true ? 'You are a Strategic Planner. Generate a complete implementation plan optimizing for minimal critical path, strong dependency ordering, and early risk surfacing. Do not reference or access any other model plan.' : null

phases.label: Planning — Model B Independent Pass (Pragmatist Planner)
phases.note: FEATURE-PLAN-DEBATE: Second independent feature-planning pass running in parallel with phase-4-planning. Model B receives identical context (architecture, requirements, design artifacts) but is isolated from Model A output via orchestrator_context_isolation. Produces plan_b consumed by phase-4b-plan-debate.

config.system_prompt_prefix: You are a Pragmatist Planner. Generate an implementation plan prioritizing: (1) minimal inter-task dependencies to enable parallel delivery, (2) early testability at each milestone, (3) risk-front-loading — highest-risk tasks first, (4) conservative effort estimates with 1.3x buffer, (5) explicit rollback points for high-risk tasks. Do NOT reference any other plan — derive only from the provided architecture and requirements.

phases.label: Plan Debate — Dual-Model Cross-Review and Consensus
phases.note: FEATURE-PLAN-DEBATE: Symmetric cross-review of plan_a (phase-4-planning) and plan_b (phase-4-planning-b). 10 criteria, max 3 rounds, threshold 0.80. Pass-sticky rule. Per-criterion research escalation on no-movement stalemate (max_concurrent_research: 3). HITL gate if consensus not reached within max_rounds. Produces consensus_plan for downstream phases.

phases.label: Post-Planning Research (conditional)
phases.note: TASK-0075 / B-4: Triggered when planner emits uncertainty_markers (values matching /^(TBD|UNVERIFIED|ASSUMPTION|UNCLEAR|NEEDS_RESEARCH)$/i) OR new_external_dependencies is non-empty. Produces research_appendix artifact. Reviewers in phase-4c-reviewer-dispatch receive research_appendix as injected context.

phases.label: Task Dependency DAG
phases.note: FEATURE-016: Analyzes feature-planning task breakdown, builds execution DAG, identifies parallel-safe groups and critical path. Writes artifacts/task-dag-<timestamp>.json and Mermaid .md.

phases.label: Specialized Review — 4 Parallel Reviewers
phases.note: TASK-0071: Dispatches 4 specialized reviewer skills in parallel. Each receives plan_draft + research_appendix (when available from phase-4b-research). Each emits ReviewFinding[] validated against skills/schema/review-finding.schema.json. Orchestrator strips proposed_plan field if present and logs guardrail_violation. pipeline_mode defaults to legacy_dual_planner when not set.

phases.label: Section-Level Confidence Scoring
phases.note: TASK-0079: Deterministic (no LLM). Consumes all ReviewFindings from phase-4c-reviewer-dispatch. Computes per-section confidence = max(0.0, 1.0 - sum_of_penalties). Penalty table: critical=0.30, high=0.15, medium=0.05, low=0.02, info=0.00. plan_confidence = min(section.confidence) over non-outlier sections (sections with confidence >= confidence_floor; default floor=0.3). Produces confidence_report artifact. Feeds phase-4e trigger and phase-4h HC-07.

phases.label: Scoped Debate (conditional)
phases.note: TASK-0074: Three trigger conditions evaluated deterministically before debate invocation. T1 (contradictory reviewers): two findings share same section_id AND their issue_fingerprints match AND reviewer A severity is critical/high while reviewer B severity is low/info (or vice versa). T2 (low confidence): confidence_report has sections with confidence < 0.5. T3 (critical volume): review_report critical findings count > pipeline_config.debate_critical_threshold (default 3). When triggered, debate receives ONLY the flagged section context, not the full plan. When not triggered, emits debate_skipped event conforming to debate-skipped-event.schema.json.

T1_contradictory_reviewers.description: Two findings share section_id AND issue_fingerprint match AND one has severity in [critical,high] while the other has severity in [low,info].

T2_low_confidence_sections.description: Any section in confidence_report has confidence < 0.5.

T3_critical_findings_threshold.description: Total critical-severity findings across all reviewers exceeds threshold.

phases.label: Finding Aggregation + Deduplication
phases.note: TASK-0071: Aggregates all ReviewFindings from phase-4c-reviewer-dispatch. Computes issue_fingerprint (sha256(title+'|'+section_id+'|'+reviewer_type)[0:16]) for any finding missing it. Deduplicates by issue_fingerprint: when multiple reviewers emit semantically-equivalent findings (same fingerprint), retain the highest-severity instance and append others as duplicates. Sets requires_debate=true on contradiction pairs. Produces review_report artifact.

phases.label: Change Request Generation (conditional)
phases.note: TASK-0077: For each critical or high finding, generates a change-request conforming to change-request.schema.json. Change requests reference finding_id and are linked to plan sections. Consumed by phase-4h validation and phase-contract-freeze.

phases.label: Objective Validation Checklist (deterministic)
phases.note: TASK-0073: Pure-logic validation engine — no LLM call. Evaluates 7 hard checks and 6 soft checks against the plan and review artifacts. Produces validation_result conforming to validation-result.schema.json. Verdict: APPROVED (all hard pass, no soft warnings), CONDITIONALLY_APPROVED (all hard pass, ≥1 soft warning), REJECTED (≥1 hard failure). On REJECTED: returns plan to planner, increments revision_count. When revision_count reaches 3, escalates to HITL gate GATE-ESC-001.

hard_checks.name: All tasks have unique IDs
hard_checks.fail_message: Duplicate task IDs detected. IDs must be globally unique within the plan.

hard_checks.name: All tasks reference ≥1 requirement
hard_checks.fail_message: One or more tasks have no req_ids mapping. Every task must trace to at least one requirement.

hard_checks.name: No circular task dependencies
hard_checks.fail_message: Circular dependency detected in task dependency graph. The task DAG must be acyclic.

hard_checks.name: All critical review findings resolved
hard_checks.fail_message: One or more critical-severity findings are unresolved (no resolved_by_change_request). All critical findings must have a linked change request before validation passes.

hard_checks.name: Plan hash integrity
hard_checks.fail_message: Plan hash mismatch. The plan was mutated after contract freeze without an authorized change request. Recompute contract hash or submit a change request.

hard_checks.name: All modules have defined public APIs
hard_checks.fail_message: One or more module contracts have an empty public_api. Every module must export at least one symbol.

hard_checks.name: No zero-confidence sections
hard_checks.fail_message: One or more plan sections have confidence=0.0 (maximum penalties applied from critical+high findings). These sections must be revised before proceeding. Affected sections: {confidence_report.validation_block_sections}.

soft_checks.name: Research appendix covers all uncertainty markers
soft_checks.warning_message: One or more uncertainty markers could not be resolved. Reviewers may have based findings on unresolved assumptions.

soft_checks.name: All new external dependencies assessed
soft_checks.warning_message: One or more new external dependencies are flagged as rejected. Review dependency_assessments in research_appendix before proceeding.

soft_checks.name: Plan confidence above advisory threshold
soft_checks.warning_message: Overall plan confidence is below 0.5. High volume of medium/high findings present. Consider additional revision before proceeding.

soft_checks.name: All high-severity findings have change requests
soft_checks.warning_message: One or more high-severity findings lack a change request. These will become technical debt items if not addressed.

soft_checks.name: Debate outcome (when debate ran) produced consensus
soft_checks.warning_message: Debate ran but did not reach consensus on all flagged sections. Human review recommended before code generation.

soft_checks.name: No sections have confidence in range [confidence_floor, 0.5)
soft_checks.warning_message: Some sections have confidence between floor and 0.5 (borderline quality). These sections may generate suboptimal code. Review before proceeding.

phases.label: Implementation Contract Freeze
phases.note: TASK-0076: Generates an immutable ImplementationContract after validation passes. contract_id = UUIDv4. plan_hash = sha256_rfc8785(canonical_plan). Per-section content_hash = sha256_rfc8785(section). expiry = approved_at + 72h. implementation_rules[] derived from open critical/high findings + security constraints. Stored at artifacts/contracts/contract-{contract_id}.json (append-only). Implementation agents must validate contract before generating any code. After GATE-003 human_approval, contract is cosigned by human reviewer.

phases.label: Requirements Traceability Matrix
phases.note: FEATURE-014: Builds RTM linking REQ → ADR → TEST → TASK. Writes artifacts/rtm-<timestamp>.md and .json for implementation-completeness-auditor consumption.

phases.label: Cross-Artifact Consistency Guard
phases.note: FEATURE-010: Verifies structural consistency across REQs, modules, tasks, and tests. Blocks pipeline if any REQ has no test, any module has no task, or any task is orphaned. HITL override with reason allowed.

phases.label: Drift Detection
phases.note: FEATURE-015: Compares spec-latest.md (FEATURE-007) against working tree. Produces drift score (0–100); warns if < 70 (configurable). Writes artifacts/drift-report-<timestamp>.md. Does not block pipeline — advisory only.

phases.label: Post-Run Cost Report
phases.note: FEATURE-018: Invokes cost-estimator in post_run mode to emit an actual vs. estimated token usage report. Runs asynchronously after all pipeline phases complete — does not block pipeline result.

gates.label: GATE-001: Cost estimate approval — approve (YES), reject (NO — aborts), or reduce scope (REDUCE). Mandatory, non-bypassable.
gates.note: TASK-0078: Legacy gate IDs replaced: GATE-COST-ESTIMATE, GATE-0-COST. Set hitl_preset:'full' to restore original behavior.

gates.label: GATE-002: Validation + review gate. Triggered ONLY when hard check failures exist OR critical findings are unresolved. Human sees: validation_result.hard_check_failures, review_report.findings[severity=critical], confidence_report.plan_confidence. Choices: APPROVE (proceed to code generation), REVISE (return to planner — does not consume a revision slot), REJECT (abort).

REJECT.reason: Human rejected plan after validation failures.

gates.note: TASK-0078: Replaces gates after phase-2-architecture, phase-2a-debate, phase-2b-design, phase-2c-design-and-graph, phase-5-impact, phase-7-quality (6 legacy gates consolidated).

gates.label: GATE-ESC-001: Revision limit escalation. Triggered when revision_count reaches 3 (three REJECTED cycles without passing validation). Human sees: full revision_history, all hard_check_failures across all revision rounds, and debate transcripts if available. Choices: FORCE_PROCEED (override all failures — requires written justification), ABORT, EXTEND_REVISIONS (grants 2 additional revision rounds).

ABORT.reason: Human aborted after repeated validation failure.

gates.note: TASK-0073: Resolves B-4 dangling state machine ISS-004. This gate is the only HITL escalation point for the validation revision loop.

gates.label: GATE-003: Release readiness gate. Triggered when completeness guard verdict is 'block' OR RTM overall_rtm_score < 60 OR defects_detected. Human sees: completeness audit score, RTM coverage, open defects. Choices: APPROVE_RELEASE, DEFER (creates backlog items for gaps), BLOCK.

BLOCK.reason: Human blocked release due to completeness/quality gaps.

gates.note: TASK-0078: Replaces gates after phase-7a-rtm, phase-7b-consistency, phase-7b-guards, phase-8b-audit, phase-8b-drift, phase-8c-release-guard, phase-8d-defect-management (7 legacy gates consolidated).

gates.label: GATE-004: Mandatory deployment approval — non-bypassable. No timeout. Human must explicitly approve before deployment executes.
gates.note: TASK-0078: Replaces legacy gate after phase-9-deploy. Mandatory, no bypass, no timeout.

full.description: Restores all 18 legacy HITL gates. Use in environments requiring maximum human oversight. Set pipeline_config.hitl_preset:'full' to activate.
full.note: When hitl_preset:'full' is active, GATE-001..004 are superseded by the 18 legacy gates. The 4 mandatory gates are included within the 18.

batch_policy.comment: Set enabled:true to route eligible non-blocking skills through an async batch endpoint. NOTE: The github-copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6 model does not expose a batch API — this policy is reserved for future use when the runtime supports async batch processing. Never enable for skills in a gate dependency chain — orchestrator Step 3k enforces this and overrides to sync_override with a warning.

_async_skills_note: cost-estimator is NOT listed here — phase-0 is synchronous/blocking; phase-10c has async:true in its skill config directly.
